notes:
hyundaiusa.com/us/en/vehicles/<model> consumer site. EV performance variants (Ioniq 5 N, Ioniq 6 N) have separate pages. Nexo is fuel cell — limited California-only availability. Session B (2026-05-14): the 2026 Nexo is a full redesign whose consumer page is not yet live — hyundaiusa.com/us/en/vehicles/nexo currently 302s to the homepage. nexo repointed to Hyundai's own press site (hyundainews.com) 2026 Nexo overview page; revisit once the consumer page launches.

Phase-3 image-scrape (2026-05-14 session 6): hyundai sits at 0% coverage and the diagnosis is structural, not a slug-variant gap. Slug-matching is healthy (tucson 45/56, elantra 21/32, ioniq-5 33/48, santa-fe 41/52 candidates contain the model slug). The failure mode is the angle-pattern stage in pickBestForAngle: Hyundai's scene7 URLs name images by internal numeric codes (2025-tucson-nx4-0272, 2026-ioniq-5-0901, 2024-santa-fe-mx5-0147, …), section labels (vlp-hero, media-slider, vert-tabs, vertical-tabs), or feature names (bluelink, digital-key, wifi-hotspot) — they NEVER carry an English angle word (front / rear / side / profile / interior / dashboard / cockpit). Alt text is uniformly the literal string "placeholder" or empty. So the angle regexes in scrape_image_urls.mjs match zero candidates and 0 images are written even though the slug filter accepts dozens per page. Genesis works on the same s7d1.scene7.com/is/image/hyundai/ CDN because Genesis's filenames carry descriptive English ("front-driver-angle-studio", "profile-passenger-studio"); Hyundai's consumer site does not.

Config-only escape hatches are not available — neither slug_variants nor path_blacklist_regex can drive an angle match. Fixing hyundai requires either (a) a per-brand angle-pattern injector in the shared script, (b) an `angle_url_patterns` brand-config key, or (c) accepting low/zero coverage. Path_blacklist tightened in this session to drop the obvious junk classes (future-vehicles concept teasers, hyundai-home solar panels, dkey2touch / digital-key feature shots) so the signal-to-noise improves for any follow-up; this does not lift coverage on its own. See reports/hyundai_phase3_investigation.md.
